content.text:
<user_query>
@Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-pt1-prototype/src/mock-prototype-auth/PrototypeCard.tsx please make sure this prototype card supports the dark theme as well.  
Make sure the colors adjust correctly to dark theme settings. 
</user_query>

Reading `PrototypeCard.tsx` and checking how the project handles dark theme tokens.

[REDACTED]

Checking how `Card` applies backgrounds and whether `CardDescription` needs tweaks.

[REDACTED]

Identifying the issue: `bg-brand-accent-50` uses a fixed scale value that stays very light in dark mode, while `border-prototype` resolves to `--brand-accent-950` in dark mode—nearly invisible on dark cards. Replacing the wash with a `color-mix` of `--prototype` and `--card` (both theme-aware) and improving dark-mode border contrast.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string:
<Card
      className={cn(
        "relative gap-0 border-2 bg-brand-accent-50 border-dashed border-prototype shadow-none ring-0",
        className,
      )}
    >
input.new_string:
<Card
      className={cn(
        "relative gap-0 border-2 border-dashed border-prototype shadow-none ring-0",
        /* Tinted surface from semantic tokens so light/dark both read as a subtle prototype wash */
        "bg-[color-mix(in_oklch,var(--prototype)_14%,var(--card))]",
        /* In dark, --prototype is very dark; use foreground hue for a visible dashed frame */
        "dark:border-prototype-foreground/40",
        className,
      )}
    >

content.text:
Here is what was wrong and what changed.

**What was off**
- `bg-brand-accent-50` always uses the same light teal from the scale, so in dark mode the frame stayed **paper-bright** on a dark page.
- `border-prototype` uses `--prototype`, which in `.dark` is `--brand-accent-950` (almost black), so the dashed edge was **barely visible** on `--card` (neutral-800).

**What we did**
1. **Surface** — Use a single wash derived from theme tokens: `color-mix(in oklch, var(--prototype) 14%, var(--card))`. In light mode that reads as a light teal tint on the card; in dark mode `--prototype` and `--card` both track the dark palette, so the fill stays in family with the rest of the UI.
2. **Border in dark** — Keep `border-prototype` for light, and add `dark:border-prototype-foreground/40` so the dashed line uses the light teal “on prototype” token in dark mode, where it actually reads as a border.

The demo badge and `text-prototype-foreground` on the title were already wired to `--prototype` / `--prototype-foreground`, which your theme switches under `.dark`; only the hardcoded background and the border contrast needed fixes in this component.

```44:56:/Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-pt1-prototype/src/mock-prototype-auth/PrototypeCard.tsx
    <Card
      className={cn(
        "relative gap-0 border-2 border-dashed border-prototype shadow-none ring-0",
        /* Tinted surface from semantic tokens so light/dark both read as a subtle prototype wash */
        "bg-[color-mix(in_oklch,var(--prototype)_14%,var(--card))]",
        /* In dark, --prototype is very dark; use foreground hue for a visible dashed frame */
        "dark:border-prototype-foreground/40",
        className,
      )}
    >
```

Note: `bun run lint` in `ui-pt1-prototype` still reports an unused `FieldLabel` import in `MockPrototypePasswordlessLoginForm.tsx`; that predates this edit. I can remove it in a follow-up if you want that package clean.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Commit **`acba098`** is on `main` with only `packages/ui-pt1-prototype/src/mock-prototype-auth/PrototypeCard.tsx` included, per the git-commits policy (one logical change, Conventional Commits).

**Message**

```
fix(ui-pt1-prototype): theme PrototypeCard for dark mode

Replace the fixed brand-accent-50 fill with a mix of --prototype and --card so the wash tracks light and dark tokens. In dark mode, draw the dashed border with prototype-foreground at partial opacity because --prototype is too dark to read against the card.
```

Your other working tree changes (onboarding app, ui-certification, ui-lib, metrics) are still unstaged so they are not part of this commit. Say if you want those split into separate commits too.
